a:r.a:t: Skills are the system's way of injecting domain-specific guidance into agent behavior at runtime without changing code. Think of a skill as a markdown document with YAML frontmatter that gets loaded into an agent's context when it is relevant to the current task. There are 3 skill kinds: retrievable skills are guidance documents indexed for semantic search, executable skills contain runnable instructions, and hybrid skills combine both. The system has 49 skill packs organized across 7 subdirectories - one per agent type - covering everything from data analysis patterns to RAG strategies. The lifecycle works in four stages. First, SkillPackFile validates the YAML frontmatter and computes a SHA256 checksum for integrity tracking. Second, SkillIndexSync discovers markdown files, chunks them for RAG indexing, and builds an inverted index - incremental sync by checksum means only changed skills get re-indexed. Third, SkillResolver does semantic retrieval using BM25 and embedding search, filtering by agent_scope and tags, with pinned skills getting priority. Fourth, SkillRuntime merges the query, matched skills, and session context within a 4000-character budget with access control filtering. Skills run in two execution contexts: inline runs in the agent's process, fork runs in isolation. Five effort levels - empty string meaning default, low, medium, high, and xhigh - control how much compute a skill execution is allowed. The telemetry module tracks an 80 percent success SLO with a 20-use review window, and the dependency graph module uses DFS cycle detection to validate skill dependencies.
